case_name: Yves Hector Vimegnon v. Gallagher Bassett Services, Inc.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited 'Abbott v. Washington County,' which the Court could not locate and found to be a non-existent/mis-cited authority; Court warned that fabricated authority may justify sanctions.

case_name: Edward Reynolds v. Progressive Technologies, Inc.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited multiple district-court decisions to argue that courts routinely strike overlength briefs; the Court found those cited cases either do not exist or are misattributed.

case_name: Nicole Olbera, et al. v. Tiara Sykes
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Appellee's pro se brief included a citation to a seemingly non-existent case; the court noted the citation appeared to be fabricated and an editor's note removed links to invalid citations in the official opinion.

case_name: Shaun Fleck v. Del-One Federal Credit Union
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited 'FutureGrowth Corp. v. SEC, 955 F. Supp. 3d 441 (S.D.N.Y. 2025)'; the Court and defendant could not locate the case and the Court found the citation fabricated (Westlaw only showed Fleck's own filing).

case_name: Beedemariam Kassaw v. Wal-Mart Corporation
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff's January 2026 memorandum/sur-reply (Dkt. 79) included AI-generated 'hallucinated cases'—fabricated case citations; Court found apparent use of AI and ordered Dkt. 79 stricken.

case_name: Randel Edward Page, Jr. v. Apperson Crump
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Appellant relied on a second nonexistent case citation in his motion for summary reversal; the Court's research confirmed the citation does not exist. || Fabricated: Case Law | Appellant relied on a nonexistent case citation in his motion for summary reversal; the Court's research confirmed the citation does not exist. || Fabricated: Case Law | Appellant relied on a third nonexistent or misrepresentative case citation in his motion for summary reversal; the Court's research confirmed the citation was erroneous/nonexistent.

case_name: 76 Route 6 Holdings Inc. v. Town of Yorktown, NY
hallucination_items: False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff claimed McEachin v. McGuinnis contains language applying 'special solicitude' to civil rights plaintiffs; the Court determined McEachin contains no such language and admonished counsel. || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff attributed to Goel v. Bunge the language that a defendant 'may not rely on statements in the documents for the truth of the matter asserted.' The Court found Goel contains no such language and admonished counsel.

case_name: Jacobs v. Payward, Inc.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited "DOE V. SMITH, No. 3:22-cv-01987 (D. Conn. 2023)", which the Court determined to be a hallucinated (nonexistent) case citation and flagged as undermining the credibility of the filings.

case_name: Danuta Dec v. Homeland Security
hallucination_items: False Quotes: Case Law | Opening brief included a false quotation (an imaginary quote) attributed in support of the standard of review; court found the quotation was inaccurate and likely AI-generated. || Fabricated: Case Law | Opening brief cited a non-existent case (first instance); court identified it as a fabricated citation likely from AI and counsel could not explain its source. || Fabricated: Case Law | Opening brief cited a second non-existent case; court treated this as another fabricated citation and noted counsel's inability to account for it.

case_name: Megan E. Bryan v. Child Support Enforcement Agency, State of Hawaiʻi
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Petitioner cited non-existent 'State v. Burdett' as a 1981 opinion at 63 Haw. 357; the actual case at that citation is State v. Masaniai, 63 Haw. 354, 628 P.2d 1018 (1981), which does not address family court jurisdiction. Court found the citation fabricated and unsupported. || Fabricated: Case Law | Petition included four additional inaccurate or non-existent case citations that did not support the propositions for which they were cited; court characterized these as hallucinated citations.

case_name: Lauren K. Morgan v. Michael P. Whitticom
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Defendant cited 'Douchette v. Doucette, 50 Conn. App. 541, 544-45 (1998)' for the proposition that property distributions may be modified; court found the reporter citation corresponds to a different opinion (Glass v. Peter Mitchell Construction Leasing and Development Corporation, 50 Conn. App. 539 (1998)) and concluded the citation is an AI-generated hallucination. || Fabricated: Case Law | Multiple other fictitious or inapplicable cases and legal propositions in the defendant's motion (notably pages 2–3); court found most cited cases do not support the asserted propositions or do not exist.
summary: The court found the defendant's post-judgment motion likely generated with AI assistance and containing numerous fictitious or inapplicable citations. The court identified specific miscitation(s) and characterized the submission as 'riddled with fake citations and legal proposition[s].' The court declined to impose a sanction now but warned the pro se defendant that future similar filings may result in sanctions under Connecticut Practice Book § 4-2(b).

case_name: Amtrust North America o/b/o Justin McGinness v. Liberty Mutual Insurance Company
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Counsel cited this non-existent Appellate Division case to support the carrier's right to subrogation against UIM benefits; court found it does not appear in the NJ Superior Court Reports. || Fabricated: Case Law | Counsel cited this nonexistent Law Division decision (and purported affirmance) to support subrogation; court found no record of the case in NJ reports. || Fabricated: Case Law | Counsel cited this purported 2006 Appellate Division case; court determined the cited authority does not exist in the NJ Superior Court Reports. || Fabricated: Case Law | Counsel cited this alleged 1971 Law Division decision and its purported affirmance; court found no such opinions in the NJ Superior Court Reports.

case_name: Patrick C. Shaltry, I v. Brent Benzing, et al.
hallucination_items: False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff attributed the sentence "A conviction is not final until the completion of the direct appeal." to People v. Swafford; the Court found the quote does not appear in the opinion and noted cited passages contradict the proposition. || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff attributed the sentence "A judgment is not final while an appeal of right remains pending." to People v. Kennedy; the Court found the quote does not appear in the opinion. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited "People v. Smith, 489 Mich. 292, 311 (2011)" for the proposition "A conviction pending on direct appeal is not a final adjudication." The Court was unable to locate the cited case and treated the citation as fabricated. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited "Savin v. City of Cleveland, 2022 WL 3715087 (6th Cir. 2022)"; the Court found the Westlaw citation pointed to an unrelated brief and could not locate a case by that name, indicating a likely mis-citation or fabrication.

case_name: Andrea K. Tantaros v. Fox News Network, LLC, et al.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited 'Roe v. Bernabei & Katz, PLLC, 314 F. Supp. 3d 200,209 (D.D.C. 2018)' as analogous authority; Court identified this citation as inaccurate/non-existent. || Fabricated: Case Law | In the sur-reply, plaintiff inaccurately cited 'Torres v. City of New York, No. 20-CV-3384, 2021 WL 3725985, at 8 (S.D.N.Y. Aug. 23, 2021)' for the proposition that words and threats can constitute actionable violence; Court marked the citation inaccurate. || Fabricated: Legal Norm | Plaintiff quoted non-existent statutory language in the GMVA, asserting it 'expressly applies to' assault, sexual misconduct, sexual abuse, and other forms of gender-based violence; Court found the quoted statutory language non-existent. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited a non-existent case 'M.D. v. New York State Off For People with Developmental Disabilities, 2023 WL 3404357, at *10 (S.D.N.Y. 2023)' to support that verbal, psychological, and physical abuse are actionable under the GMVA; Court identified it as non-existent/inaccurate. || Fabricated: Case Law | In her sur-reply, plaintiff inaccurately cited 'Garcia v. Comprehensive Ctr., LLC, No. 21-CV-6945, 2022 WL 2237452, at 4-5 (S.D.N.Y. June 22, 2022)'; Court found the citation inaccurate. || Fabricated: Legal Norm | Quoted non-existent statutory language attributed to the GMVA; court found the quoted language does not appear in the statute. || Fabricated: Case Law | Cited a non-existent case presented as supporting that verbal, psychological, and physical abuse are actionable under the GMVA. || False Quotes: Case Law | Attributed a quoted holding to Roe in support of coverage for 'harassing comments, threats, and gestures of a sexual nature'—court flagged as an inaccurate citation/quotation in the filing. || Fabricated: Case Law | Cited Garcia v. Comprehensive Ctr., LLC with specific pages and holdings as support for physical and verbal acts being covered—court identified the citation as inaccurate. || Fabricated: Case Law | Cited Torres v. City of New York for the proposition that words and threats can constitute actionable violence; court identified the citation as inaccurate in the filing.

case_name: Pizzimenti v. City of Detroit, et al.
hallucination_items: Misrepresented: Case Law | Plaintiff cited Opulent Life Church, 697 F.3d 279 in support of fines/Eighth Amendment/retaliation arguments, but the court noted that Opulent Life Church does not involve fines nor those claims and was thus misapplied. || Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited 'Williams v. City of Cleveland, No. 1:18-CV-2911, 2019 WL 5485190 (N.D. Ohio Oct. 25, 2019),' which the court and R&R found does not exist in Westlaw or under that docket. || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff quoted language attributed to Jensen v. Utah Cnty., 2025 WL 2208257 (D. Utah Aug. 4, 2025), but the quoted passage does not appear in that opinion. || Fabricated: Case Law | Complaint referenced 'Thaxton v. City of Pontiac, No. 298862, 2011 WL 522951 (Mich. Ct. App. Feb. 15, 2011),' a citation the court says does not exist. || Fabricated: Case Law | Complaint referenced 'Smith v. State, 26 N.E.3d 1167 (Ind. 2015),' which the court was unable to locate or identify as relevant.

case_name: McCarthy v. U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | One cited DEA adjudication in the Opening Brief was entirely fabricated (did not exist); discovered when the Court ordered copies of the cited authorities. || Misrepresented: Case Law | Seven DEA-adjudication summaries were riddled with factual and legal inaccuracies and misrepresented the adjudications (e.g., portrayed lesser sanctions than actually imposed); the summaries were AI-generated and not verified by counsel.
outcome: Reprimand; Notification to other courts and the National Disciplinary Data Bank

case_name: The Advocates for Human Rights and L.H.M. v. U.S. Department of Homeland Security, et al.
hallucination_items: False Quotes: Case Law | Brief riddled with misquotations

case_name: Curry v. Capital One Auto Finance
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Legal Norm | Proposed amended complaint asserted fictitious provisions of a purported North Carolina Identity Theft Protection Act (ITPA); the court identified these statutory provisions as fabricated and tied them to likely AI drafting. || Fabricated: Case Law | Second proposed amended complaint contained fabricated case citations; the court found the inclusion of fabricated citations constituted bad faith and a basis to deny leave to amend.
summary: The court found the plaintiff's proposed amended complaints included fabricated legal authorities — including fictitious provisions of a purported North Carolina Identity Theft Protection Act and other fabricated citations — and concluded the filings were likely drafted with AI. The court denied leave to amend as futile and constituted bad faith due to the fabricated citations, and granted defendant's motion to dismiss.

case_name: Baker v. Rastelli Foods LLC
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Multiple cases cited by plaintiffs were so inaccurately cited that the Court was unable to locate them and questioned whether they exist. || False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiffs used a quotation that does not appear in the cited case (false quotation noted by the Court).
outcome: Order to provide PDF copies of cited case; Order to Show Cause

case_name: Anthony Wallace v. PennyMac Loan Services, LLC, et al.
hallucination_items: Fabricated: Case Law | Court found the cited 'Shapiro v JPMorgan Chase & Co., No. 1:13-cv-09764-PGG, 2014 WL 1224546' does not correspond to any case; SDNY docket shows the number not associated with that name. || Fabricated: Case Law | Court found 'Bally v. Home Loan Servicing, LP, No. 2:09-cv-02445-MCE-KJM, 2011 WL 867568' does not bring up a case by that name and the case number is assigned to a different matter. || Fabricated: Case Law | Court found 'Pasillas v. HSBC Bank USA, N.A., No. 2:12-cv-01107, 2013 WL 3873227' cited in plaintiff's reply does not correspond to a case by that name and the case number is assigned to a different case in this court. || Fabricated: Case Law | Court found 'Boyle v. Bank of America, N.A., No. 2:13-cv-02021-APG-VCF, 2015 WL 1321435' does not correspond to any case by that name and the case number is assigned to a different case in this court. || Fabricated: Case Law | Court found 'Holmes v. Countrywide Financial Corp., No. 2:10-cv-00333-RCJ-PAL, 2010 WL 11597538' does not bring up a case by that name and the number refers to another matter in this court. || False Quotes: Case Law | Court found plaintiff cites Jolley at 213 Cal. App. 4th 872, 905 (2013) for a quoted passage, but the opinion at that citation (Jolley v. Chase Home Finance, LLC) does not contain the quoted language. || False Quotes: Case Law | Court found plaintiff purports to quote Weingartner v. Chase Home Finance, LLC, 702 F. Supp. 2d 1276, 1288 (D. Nev. 2010), but the quoted language does not appear in that opinion.
